description_long: This object is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. The barrier is trapezoidal in shape, with a wide base for stability and a narrower top, featuring two rusted metal lifting hooks embedded on the upper surface. The surface is weathered and stained, showing signs of age and exposure, with visible cracks and a rough, matte finish. A prominent yellow and black diagonal hazard stripe runs horizontally along the lower portion of both main faces, serving as a visual warning for vehicles and pedestrians. The ends of the barrier are plain, with some geometric recesses and the continuation of the weathered concrete texture. The overall appearance suggests durability and functionality, designed to withstand impacts and outdoor conditions.
description: A weathered concrete jersey barrier with rusted metal hooks and yellow-black hazard stripes, showing signs of outdoor wear and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face with a yellow-black hazard stripe and two rusted metal hooks on top.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow end, geometric recesses, and the edge of the hazard stripe.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the hazard stripe and hooks visible, but mirrored.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow end and the edge of the hazard stripe.
